items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58252 by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58252 added several significant features and breaking changes to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`: - **Chat isolation key enforcement** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header): all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for a response must include the same key used at creation; mismatch returns 404 - **Metadata constraint validation**: maps limited to 16 keys, key length Ōēż 64, value length Ōēż 512 - **Malformed response ID validation** for both the `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs outbound Foundry storage API calls with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers - **Breaking changes to error shapes**: POST-delete operations now return 404 (was 400), `invalid_request_error` type for 400 errors, cancel-after-delete returns 404 - **HttpClient instrumentation** added to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` for outbound trace correlation ## Gaps Found - **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not documented anywhere in the README. The `ResponseContext.Isolation` property is mentioned once without explanation. Users need to understand when and how to use this header for multi-tenant scenarios - **Metadata constraints** (16 keys max, 64-char keys, 512-char values) are not mentioned in the README. Users will get 400 errors without any hint of what limits apply - **Response ID format requirements** are not documented. Users creating custom integrations need to know that IDs must match a specific format (`caresp_` prefix with exact length) or requests will be rejected with 400 - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** is not documented in the README Logging section. Users building production deployments need to know this policy exists and what it logs - **Breaking changes in error shapes** are not reflected in the README Troubleshooting section. The 400 ŌåÆ 404 change for post-delete scenarios and the updated error type names will surprise users upgrading from beta.1 - **OpenTelemetry / HttpClient instrumentation** added to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` is not reflected in the Responses README observability guidance
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` (edit) Add a new **Chat Isolation** subsection under **Key concepts**, immediately after the **ResponseContext** subsection: ```markdown ### Chat Isolation When a response is created with the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A mismatched or missing key returns **404 Not Found**, indistinguishable from a response that does not exist. This prevents cross-chat information leakage in multi-tenant deployments. ```csharp // Creating with a chat isolation key (set the header on the client request) // x-agent-chat-isolation-key: // ResponseContext.Isolation contains the key extracted from the inbound request var isolationKey = context.Isolation?.IsolationKey; ``` No enforcement applies to responses created without this header. ``` --- Add a **Metadata constraints** note to the **Key concepts ŌåÆ ResponseContext** section (or as its own subsection): ```markdown ### Metadata constraints Metadata maps attached to a response must satisfy: - At most **16 key-value pairs** - Key length Ōēż **64 characters** - Value length Ōēż **512 characters** Requests that exceed these limits are rejected with **400 Bad Request**. ``` --- Add an **Input validation** subsection under **Key concepts**: ```markdown ### Input validation Response IDs (both the `response_id` path parameter and the `previous_response_id` body field) must conform to the expected format (`caresp_` prefix, fixed total length). Malformed IDs are rejected with **400 Bad Request** and a descriptive error message. ``` --- Update the **Logging** subsection under **Troubleshooting** to mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`: Replace the existing logging paragraph with: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues. Outbound calls to the Foundry storage layer are logged by `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, a built-in Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy. Each attempt is logged with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Successful calls are logged at `Information` level; HTTP errors at `Warning`; exceptions at `Error`. No credentials or PII are included. The `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package also registers `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()` for OpenTelemetry, exporting outbound HTTP client spans so distributed traces include the full call chain through the Foundry storage layer. ``` --- Update the **Troubleshooting ŌåÆ Common errors** section to reflect the beta.2 breaking changes: Replace the existing bullet points with: ```markdown ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request** (creation/update): The request body failed validation. Possible causes: - `input` items are malformed or `model` is invalid - `previous_response_id` does not match the expected ID format - Metadata map exceeds 16 keys, or a key/value exceeds the length limits (64 / 512 characters) - Response ID in path parameter is malformed - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired, or was deleted. This status is also returned when a chat isolation key is present and mismatched (intentionally indistinguishable from not-found). - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). - **404 Not Found** (cancel or GET after delete): The response was deleted, created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for the changes described above; they are prose descriptions of behavior and constraints. The existing snippet infrastructure does not need updating. If a sample demonstrating chat isolation is desired in the future, it should be placed in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample_ChatIsolation.cs` with a `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion` block. The corresponding README placeholder would be: ``` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in correct order: 1. Ō£ģ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. Ō£ģ Key concepts ŌĆö needs the three new subsections (Chat Isolation, Metadata constraints, Input validation) 3. Ō£ģ Examples 4. ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Troubleshooting ŌĆö Logging and Common errors subsections need updates 5. Ō£ģ Next steps 6. Ō£ģ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö confirm README is unchanged (no new snippet placeholders added in this PR) 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` No public API surface was changed by this documentation-only update, so `Export-API.ps1` is not needed.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document chat isolation, metadata constraints, ID validation, and observability features added in beta.2
